rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. LANUOLA AFOA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSECUTE is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 940 GUERRERO, LLC VS. CALVIN G. MARKSMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2023 line 6. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND SANCTIONS Hearing Required. No later than 8:30 a.m. on August 16, 2023 Defendant's counsel to file and serve a supplemental brief (courtesy copies to be delivered to department 501 by 8:45 a.m. on August 16, 2023) not to exceed 5 pages addressing: (1) applicability of CCP 2025.450(g)(1) (Opposition 2:15) to this motion made pursuant to CCP 2030.290 and 3031.300 (Notice of Motion 2:1-2); (2) case authority for the proposition that "A party seeking discovery sanctions must demonstrate that the opposing party's failure to answer deposition questions were "substantially justified." (Opposition 2:13-14), i.e. that it is the moving party's burden to establish that responding party's failure to respond was substantially justified; and (3) identify the location of evidence that: "Defendant provided late responses due to a number of unanticipated delays, including a recent visit to the emergency room as the result of an injury for facial fractures." (Opposition 2:18-19) by the title of the document on file as of the time of the filing of the opposition, page and line number, e.g. Declaration of Jane Doe filed on August 15, 2023 at 9:54 a.m., page 2, lines 3-4. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE WON ET AL VS. ALAN KELLY WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2023 line 9. DEFENDANT ALAN WONG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to clearly identify who the Plaintiffs are. Based on the current allegations in paragraph 1 of the Complain it appears that the plaintiffs are (1) Stephanie Won; (2) Corey Wong; and (2) Trustees of the Wong Won Family Trust. Complaint is verified only by Stephani Won, co-Trustee of the Wong Won Family Trust. Based on the opposition it appears that the Plaintiff could be: either (1) Stephanie Won, as co-Trustee and (2) Corey Wong, as co-Trustee or Stephani Won and Corey Wong in their individual capacities. Plaintiff must clearly allege who the plaintiffs are in an what capacities these persons bring the action. The complaint shall be verified accordingly. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Allegations are clear as to what Plaintiff's position regarding the applicability of TPA-2019 and SFRO are and the Defendant may admit or deny such allegations. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
